title: Phyllota squarrosa
intro: Phyllota squarrosa, also known as New Zealand tea tree or New Zealand bushman's tea, is a shrubby plant native to eastern Australia and New Zealand, belonging to the Fabaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as colds and flu, due to its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which provide relief from congestion and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Phyllota squarrosa include isoflavones, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Phyllota squarrosa is often prepared as an infusion or a tincture, and is used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
